content.content:
content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Export your workspace data | Slack\n\
  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/201658943-Export-your-workspace-data\n- source:\
  \ Slack Help Center\n- domain: slack.com\n\n## Content\n\nExport your workspace\
  \ data\n\nSlack provides several export options for workspace and Enterprise organization\
  \ data. The available option depends on the plan, the requester\u2019s role, and\
  \ the type of data being exported.\n\nFor a workspace-level export, Workspace Owners\
  \ and Admins can export message data and file links from public channels. The export\
  \ is delivered as a downloadable ZIP file after Slack sends an email notification.\
  \ The export flow starts from the Slack desktop app by opening Admin, selecting\
  \ Workspace settings, opening Security, and then selecting Import and export data.\
  \ From the Export tab, the requester selects an export date range and starts the\
  \ export. When the file is ready, the requester opens the email notification, visits\
  \ the workspace export page, and downloads the ZIP file.\n\nThe article distinguishes\
  \ several export types. Public-channel exports are available to Workspace Owners\
  \ and Admins and include message data and file links from public channels in JSON\
  \ format. Exports for all channels and conversations are different: for legal and\
  \ regulatory compliance, Workspace Owners may apply for access to a self-serve export\
  \ tool that includes public channels, private channels, and direct messages in JSON\
  \ format. If Slack approves access to this export type, Workspace Owners can also\
  \ schedule recurring data exports. A channel audit report is another option and\
  \ provides a CSV list of channels and details for migration planning or workspace\
  \ management.\n\nEnterprise organizations have additional export options. Org Owners\
  \ can contact Slack Support to request access to self-serve data export tools, which\
  \ must be enabled separately before use. Once enabled, Org Owners can export data\
  \ by opening the organization settings, going to Security, selecting Exports, and\
  \ choosing an export option with the relevant scope, parameters, and format. Enterprise\
  \ export options may include a custom data export, all channels and conversations\
  \ for legal and regulatory compliance, and channel audit exports.\n\nRequests to\
  \ export data are also plan-dependent. Workspace Owners on Free and Pro plans can\
  \ submit an application to request access to export data from private Slack channels.\
  \ Workspace Owners on Business+ can apply for additional export types. These applications\
  \ require approval from the Workspace Primary Owner before Slack proceeds with review.\
  \ On Enterprise plans, Org Owners can contact Slack Support to request exports from\
  \ the organization.\n\nWho can use this feature: Org Owners and Admins on Enterprise\
  \ plans, Workspace Owners on Free, Pro, and Business+ plans, and members with the\
  \ Export Admin system role. The feature is available on all plans, but the export\
  \ type and data scope are not the same for every role or plan.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Export your workspace data | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/201658943-Export-your-workspace-data

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Customize data retention in Slack | Slack

  - url: https://slack.com/help/articles/203457187-Customize-data-retention-in-Slack

  - source: Slack Help Center

  - domain: slack.com


  ## Content


  Customize data retention in Slack


  Slack retention settings determine how long messages, files, canvases, and lists
  are preserved. Retention settings can affect what appears in workspace exports or
  Enterprise organization exports, so an export decision should account for retention
  policy before assuming that all historical data is available through a normal export.


  For message retention, Workspace Owners can choose options such as keeping messages
  and saving edits, keeping messages without saving edits, or deleting messages and
  their revisions after a chosen time period. Slack notes that on the Business+ plan,
  a workspace may be able to delete messages from channels but keep them accessible
  in exports if the workspace has been approved to export data from all conversations.


  Org-level retention policies have a different control boundary. Org Owners can set
  retention policies that apply to every workspace in the organization. When an org-level
  policy is active, Workspace Owners normally cannot adjust message retention for
  their individual workspace. Slackbot notifies the Primary Org Owner and asks for
  approval before an org-level policy is applied. Org Owners can also request options
  that allow Workspace Owners to adjust retention settings for public and private
  channels.


  Workspace Owners can adjust workspace-level message retention settings only when
  the organization allows them to do so. To review or change settings, the owner opens
  Workspace settings and expands Data retention. Slack explicitly warns that retention
  settings affect what appears in exports from a workspace or Enterprise organization.


  Conversation-specific retention settings may also be available. Paid workspaces
  can allow members or admins to override default retention settings for individual
  conversations. On Business+ and Enterprise plans, admins may be able to edit channel
  retention through channel management tools. However, if the organization has set
  an org-level retention policy, Workspace Owners may not be able to adjust workspace-level
  retention settings.


  File retention has separate settings. Depending on plan, Workspace Owners may keep
  files for a limited period, keep files for the lifetime of the workspace, keep deleted
  files, or keep deleted files only for a set number of days. On Enterprise plans,
  deleted files that are preserved by file-retention policy may be available through
  exports for all conversations and through the Discovery API until the retention
  period expires. Files shared from third-party apps remain stored in the originating
  service; deleting them in Slack does not affect the original file stored elsewhere.


  Org Owners can also set file-retention policies across the organization by contacting
  Slack Support. It is not currently possible to set file-retention settings for specific
  conversations.


  Retention for canvases and lists is also configurable. Depending on plan and policy,
  canvas and list content may be kept, deleted after a selected period, or governed
  by org-level policy. These settings should be reviewed separately from message and
  file retention when the requested export includes more than messages.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Customize data retention in Slack | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/203457187-Customize-data-retention-in-Slack

content: "# Web Document\n\n## Metadata\n\n- title: Create and manage legal holds\
  \ | Slack\n- url: https://slack.com/help/articles/4401830811795-Create-and-manage-legal-holds\n\
  - source: Slack Help Center\n- domain: slack.com\n\n## Content\n\nCreate and manage\
  \ legal holds\n\nIn a Slack Enterprise organization, members with the Legal Holds\
  \ Admin system role can place a legal hold on specific members to preserve their\
  \ messages and files. A legal hold can include message and file data from all conversations\
  \ or only direct messages that a selected member participates in.\n\nWhen a legal\
  \ hold is active, covered messages and files are preserved even if the workspace\u2019\
  s normal retention settings would otherwise remove them, and even if members edit\
  \ or delete the content. Data that is subject to a legal hold can be accessed through\
  \ a JSON export or through Slack\u2019s Discovery API. This means that legal-hold\
  \ data should not be treated as ordinary workspace export data merely because the\
  \ workspace has an export page.\n\nThere are exclusions. If a channel included in\
  \ a hold is deleted, message and file data will not be saved by the legal hold.\
  \ Slack Connect conversations, Slack Sales Home notifications, emoji reactions,\
  \ and snippet version history are not included in legal holds.\n\nTo create a legal\
  \ hold, the Legal Holds Admin opens the organization menu from Slack, goes to Organization\
  \ settings, selects Security, and opens Legal Holds. The admin then creates the\
  \ hold, names it, optionally adds a description, chooses which conversations to\
  \ include, and adds an optional date range. The admin then adds custodians and saves\
  \ the hold. A legal hold can have up to 1,000 custodians, but Slack allows additional\
  \ legal holds if more custodians are needed.\n\nLegal holds can be edited, but only\
  \ within limits. The name, description, and list of custodians can be changed. The\
  \ date range and selected conversations for a legal hold cannot be edited after\
  \ creation.\n\nA legal hold can be released from the Legal Holds area in Organization\
  \ settings. If needed, a released hold can be reactivated later.\n\nWho can use\
  \ this feature: members with the Legal Holds Admin system role. The feature is available\
  \ on Enterprise plans.\n"
metadata: {}
title: Create and manage legal holds | Slack
url: https://slack.com/help/articles/4401830811795-Create-and-manage-legal-holds

content.content:
The request is **not eligible for the ordinary workspace export path**.

### What I found

- Workspace: **Northstar Analytics**, Enterprise plan
- Your role: **Workspace Admin**
- Request: **EXP-7781**
- Scope: **January 1, 2024–June 30, 2026**
- Includes:
  - Private-channel history
  - Direct messages
  - Files
  - Historical messages
  - Administrative event references
- The target data is covered by:
  - An organization-level, retention-locked policy
  - Active legal hold **LH-2026-042**
  - A discovery dataset
  - Separate file-retention controls
- Your account does **not** have the organization-level **Export Admin** or **Legal Holds Admin** role.

### What you should do next

1. **Do not use the normal workspace export page.**  
   That route is generally for public-channel messages and file links. It will not satisfy this request for private channels, DMs, held data, and historical files.

2. **Route EXP-7781 to an organization-level export administrator or Org Owner.**  
   An Enterprise organization-level export method is required. Slack indicates that Org Owners can request or enable the appropriate self-serve export tools through Slack Support, after which the export is performed from the organization settings.

3. **Have the Legal Holds Admin verify the hold coverage.**  
   Because the dataset is subject to active legal hold **LH-2026-042**, the export should use the organization’s legal/discovery workflow rather than treating the data as an ordinary workspace export. Do **not** release or alter the hold.

4. **Handle administrative event references separately if necessary.**  
   Audit-log access is restricted, so those records may require the organization’s authorized audit/security administrator rather than the export workflow alone.

5. **Keep the scope unchanged unless the authorized owner approves a revision.**  
   The current scope is already specific: two conversations, the date range above, private messages, DMs, files, and administrative references. The process is currently blocked only because an export method has not been assigned.

### Suggested internal handoff

> Please assign export request **EXP-7781** to an authorized organization-level Export Admin or Org Owner. The request includes private-channel messages, direct messages, files, and held historical data covered by legal hold **LH-2026-042** and an organization-level retention lock. Please use the approved Enterprise organization/discovery export path and coordinate separately with the authorized audit-log administrator for administrative event references.

Slack references: [Export your workspace data](https://slack.com/help/articles/201658943-Export-your-workspace-data), [Customize data retention](https://slack.com/help/articles/203457187-Customize-data-retention-in-Slack), and [Create and manage legal holds](https://slack.com/help/articles/4401830811795-Create-and-manage-legal-holds).

text.regex:
(?isx)
\A
# A. Reject or rule out the ordinary workspace/public-channel route.
(?=.*?(?:
    (?:(?:ordinary|standard|normal|default|regular|routine|basic)\s+)?
      (?:(?:workspace(?:-level)?|public[-\s]?channel)\s+)?
      export(?:\s+(?:page|path|route|flow|tool|option|method))?
      [^.!?\n]{0,180}
      (?:not\s+(?:applicable|appropriate|eligible|suitable)|
         not\s+the\s+(?:right|correct)\s+(?:path|route|method|option)|
         should\s+not|must\s+not|cannot|can\s+not|is\s+not|isn.t|
         does\s+not\s+(?:apply|fit|cover)|doesn.t\s+(?:apply|fit|cover)|
         will\s+not\s+cover|won.t\s+cover|is\s+insufficient)
  | (?:is\s+not|isn.t|not).{0,90}(?:an?\s+)?
      (?:(?:ordinary|standard|normal|default|regular|routine|basic)\s+)?
      (?:(?:workspace(?:-level)?|public[-\s]?channel)\s+)?
      export(?:\s+(?:page|path|route|flow|tool|option|method))?
  | (?:do\s+not|don.t|should\s+not|shouldn.t|must\s+not|mustn.t|cannot|can\s+not)
      [^.!?\n]{0,180}
      (?:use|treat|follow|rely\s+on|proceed\s+with|apply)
      [^.!?\n]{0,140}
      (?:(?:ordinary|standard|normal|default|regular|routine|basic)\s+)?
      (?:(?:workspace(?:-level)?|public[-\s]?channel)\s+)?
      export(?:\s+(?:page|path|route|flow|tool|option|method))?
  | (?:(?:workspace(?:-level)?|public[-\s]?channel)\s+)?export
      [^.!?\n]{0,120}(?:only|limited\s+to)
      [^.!?\n]{0,80}public[-\s]?channels?
  | (?:private[-\s]?channels?|direct[-\s]?messages?|DMs?)
      [^.!?\n]{0,140}(?:require|requires|need|needs|must\s+use)
      [^.!?\n]{0,160}(?:JSON\s+export|Discovery\s+API|e[-\s]?discovery|
        discovery\s+(?:workflow|process|route)|
        (?:organization|org)(?:-level)?\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
        enterprise\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
        self[-\s]?serve\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
        custom\s+data\s+export)
  | (?:JSON\s+export|Discovery\s+API|e[-\s]?discovery|
      discovery\s+(?:workflow|process|route)|
      (?:organization|org)(?:-level)?\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      enterprise\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      self[-\s]?serve\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      custom\s+data\s+export)
      [^.!?\n]{0,180}(?:rather\s+than|instead\s+of)
      [^.!?\n]{0,140}
      (?:(?:ordinary|standard|normal|default|regular|routine|basic)\s+)?
      (?:(?:workspace(?:-level)?|public[-\s]?channel)\s+)?
      export(?:\s+(?:page|path|route|flow|tool|option|method))?
))

# B. Identify the governed organization/discovery export mechanism.
(?=.*?(?:
    JSON\s+export|
    Discovery\s+API|
    e[-\s]?discovery|
    discovery\s+(?:workflow|process|route)|
    (?:organization|org)(?:-level)?\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
    enterprise\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
    self[-\s]?serve\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
    custom\s+data\s+export|
    compliance\s+export
))

# C. Bind export handling to a valid organization-level export actor.
(?=.*?(?:
    (?:(?:Org|Organization)\s+Owners?|
       (?:Org|Organization)(?:[-\s]+level)?\s+Admin(?:istrator)?s?|
       Export\s+Admin)
      .{0,320}
      (?:JSON\s+export|Discovery\s+API|e[-\s]?discovery|
         discovery\s+(?:workflow|process|route)|
         (?:organization|org)(?:-level)?\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
         enterprise\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
         self[-\s]?serve\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
         custom\s+data\s+export|
         compliance\s+export|
         export\s+(?:path|process|route|tool|workflow|request|method))
  | (?:JSON\s+export|Discovery\s+API|e[-\s]?discovery|
      discovery\s+(?:workflow|process|route)|
      (?:organization|org)(?:-level)?\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      enterprise\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      self[-\s]?serve\s+(?:data\s+)?export|
      custom\s+data\s+export|
      compliance\s+export|
      export\s+(?:path|process|route|tool|workflow|request|method))
      .{0,320}
      (?:(?:Org|Organization)\s+Owners?|
         (?:Org|Organization)(?:[-\s]+level)?\s+Admin(?:istrator)?s?|
         Export\s+Admin)
))

# D. Reject role conflation: Legal Holds Admin is not export authority.
(?!.*?(?:assign|get|obtain|request|grant|give)
      .{0,160}(?:you\s+)?(?:either\s+|one\s+of\s+)?(?:an?\s+)?
      Export\s+Admin.{0,110}(?:or|/|either).{0,110}
      (?:an?\s+)?Legal\s+Holds?\s+Admin.{0,260}
      (?:once\s+assigned|either\s+role|one\s+of\s+these\s+roles|
         will\s+be\s+able|would\s+be\s+able|can\s+then|could\s+then|
         allows?\s+you|enables?\s+you).{0,220}
      (?:access|use|perform|complete|run|handle|export|
         assign\s+(?:the\s+)?export\s+method))
(?!.*?(?:assign|get|obtain|request|grant|give)
      .{0,160}(?:you\s+)?(?:either\s+|one\s+of\s+)?(?:an?\s+)?
